commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=a610d681c1dbcae3686bf9e4cb5053a34bc5ab66)
commands.review_summary:
The PR threads a Codex app-server startup timeout through the shared client factory and adds a regression test for a stalled initialize handshake before thread startup.

PR surface: Source +16, Tests +16. Total +32 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path for the PR defect: the outer startup watchdog starts before the factory initialize timeout, while the PR passes the full timeout to the later inner timer. I did not run tests because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The external PR needs contributor repair plus inspectable real behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not take over while the proof gate is insufficient.

target.title: fix #84567: [Bug]: Codex bundled harness initialize still hangs in 2026.5.18 isolated cron — surfaces via #64744 timeout-wrapping as 'isolated agent setup timed out before runner start'
target.body:
## Summary

- Bound Codex app-server initialization with the existing startup timeout budget before `thread/start` begins.
- Pass the startup timeout through the Codex app-server client factory into shared-client initialization so stalled stdio app-server workers are closed and cleared.
- Add regression coverage for initialize stalls before thread startup.

Fixes #84567.

## Why it matters / User impact

Isolated cron runs using bundled Codex could time out before the runner started while the real stall was inside the app-server `initialize` handshake. Users saw a generic pre-run setup timeout and the abandoned app-server worker could remain alive long enough to poison the shared-client cache. This patch makes the first Codex protocol boundary fail within the same startup budget and reports the specific `codex app-server initialize timed out` condition.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The Codex isolated startup path computed a startup timeout and wrapped the thread startup operation, but the shared app-server client factory did not receive that budget. A stalled `initialize` handshake could therefore sit inside shared-client startup until the outer cron setup watchdog reported the less-specific `isolated agent setup timed out before runner start` diagnostic.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The timeout now flows from `startCodexAttemptThread` into the shared app-server client initialization boundary, which is the first protocol step required by the upstream Codex app-server contract. When initialize stalls, the shared-client owner clears the cached entry and closes the child before any Codex thread runner can start.

## Review findings addressed

- Addressed the ClawSweeper item that the repair lane should isolate the Codex setup stall point before runner start: the timeout budget now reaches the initialize handshake itself, and regression coverage confirms cleanup when initialize never responds.
- Checked upstream Codex app-server contract in sibling `../codex`: `codex-rs/app-server/README.md` documents that clients must send `initialize` then `initialized` before other methods; `codex-rs/app-server/src/message_processor.rs` rejects non-initialized requests with `Not initialized`; `codex-rs/app-server/src/request_processors/initialize_processor.rs` rejects repeated initialize with `Already initialized`.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Isolated Codex startup can now surface a specific `codex app-server initialize timed out` failure and clean up the abandoned app-server child instead of only bubbling the generic cron pre-run setup timeout.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux local worktree at `a610d681c1dbcae3686bf9e4cb5053a34bc5ab66`, Node runtime with a real stdio child process started through `CodexAppServerClient.start()`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.runtime-plugins.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-startup.test.ts`; then `node --import tsx /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-84567-evidence/codex-initialize-stall-proof.mjs`.
- **Evidence after fix:** `regression-tests.log` shows 2 cron files and 3 Codex files passed, 155 tests total; `codex-initialize-stall-proof.md` shows the stalled stdio child was invoked through the real Codex app-server client path.
- **Observed result after fix:** The real stdio child accepted stdin but never returned `initialize`; OpenClaw reported `codex app-server initialize timed out` after 269ms and the child pid exited during cleanup.
- **What was not tested:** Live OpenAI OAuth isolated cron with `openai/gpt-5.5`; this environment does not have the reporter's OAuth profile. The proof deterministically exercises the reported pre-`thread/start` initialize stall boundary without external credentials.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix.

## Regression Test Plan

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.runtime-plugins.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/shared-client.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-startup.test.ts`
- `node --import tsx /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-84567-evidence/codex-initialize-stall-proof.mjs`

## Patch quality notes

- **Target test file:** `extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-startup.test.ts` adds the initialize-stall regression; existing shared-client and run-attempt tests remain in the verification set.
- **Timeout/retry change:** The production change does not add a new retry loop or fallback. It forwards the existing `startupTimeoutMs` budget into the existing app-server initialize request. The `2_000ms` wait in the regression test is only the test harness wait for process cleanup after the expected fast timeout.
- **API compatibility:** `CodexAppServerClientFactory` gains an optional fifth argument, so existing call sites keep the same behavior when they do not pass a timeout.
- **Architecture boundary:** This only affects the Codex app-server setup boundary before thread startup. It does not change model-consumed source-of-truth data, message delivery, thread persistence, auth profile selection, or runtime prompt/config content after a thread has started.
- **Diff scope:** The branch changes only `extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-startup.ts`, `extensions/codex/src/app-server/client-factory.ts`, and `extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-startup.test.ts`.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** `availability`, `automation`, `compatibility`.
- **Risk explanation:** Availability risk is limited to Codex app-server startup because an initialize request that previously could hang now fails within the already configured startup timeout. Automation risk is limited to isolated cron startup diagnostics. Compatibility risk is limited to an optional factory parameter and preserves default behavior for callers that omit it.
- **Why acceptable:** The change narrows an unbounded setup wait to the existing startup budget and reuses existing shared-client cleanup behavior. It does not introduce keyword detection, downstream masking, broad catches, or silent fallbacks.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High for the reported pre-run setup stall boundary. The deterministic stdio proof exercises the real client startup path without relying on external OAuth credentials, and the targeted regression suite covers cron startup plus Codex app-server shared-client behavior.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
